title: Science in the Bakery
summary: Sophie the baker ventures to a science lab to join Bunsen Honeydew and Beaker for a day of experimentation. After receiving specialized laboratory goggles from her mother, she learns the secrets of color-mixing to enhance her frosting recipes. The group then visits Sophie's bakery, where she showcases her unique culinary creations, including meat-salad cupcakes and pork tortilla treats.

highlightQuote.text: I discovered that orange and blue make green... teal-y green.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Oops. Let's turn out the light.
No, I'll turn it off. 'Cause my way of turning off the lights is this way.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: There. Okay.
That's my way. Papa, tell a story about Bunsen and Beaker. Izzy, let's tell a story about Bunsen and Beaker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Ding dong Bunsen Beaker. Ding dong Bunsen Beaker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Bunsen and Beaker said, 'Hello?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Meep! Sophie!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Bunsen said, 'Oh, hello Sophie, from the Eat and Greet Bakery.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Meep meep meep meep meep.
And Bunsen said, 'Oh yes, that's our favorite, especially Beaker loves the Eat and Greet Bakery. What's your favorite thing to eat from there, Beaker?' And what did Beaker say?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Meep! That means he loves chocolate and cherry and icing and frosting cakes with cherries on top. That's Beaker's favorite things.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Bunsen said, 'Come on in, Sophie!' and what did she say?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: She said, 'Wow! Your science lab looks different than my bakery. My bakery has daisies and sunflowers, but your house does not have flowers.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Bunsen said, 'Oh yes, we have a different kind of decoration. We have a map of the world, and a human skeleton so you can see what the bones look like, and we have all kinds of bottles and jars of chemicals, and we have a place to combine the chemicals in different test tubes, and we have eye droppers and pitchers and things for mixing. Why yes, I can see how our science laboratory would look very different than your bakery. Do you like it?'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I love it!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, what brings you over to my house today? Why are you here?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: To do some science. I have a smock.
Oh good. Very important to have proper protective equipment. And do you have goggles as well? If not, we can lend you some.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I have swimming goggles at home.
Oh, we'll lend you some laboratory goggles. Those will protect your eyes in case anything splashes.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, my mom has them in her room. My mom got those laboratory goggles. Sophie asked her mom, 'Um, do you have any laboratory goggles left?' 'I have one pair. They're pink though, but they have clear with a pink frame. I believe they're all white in color, but they're clear.' Okay, so then she ran back to their house and rang the door. Ding dong!
And Bunsen said, 'Oh, you're back! Very good. Now, what kind of experiment would you like to do, Sophie?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now, I have the goggles that my mom had, and she gave me the clear kind.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh good, it's important for them to be clear so you can see through them.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Sophie's mom said she only had clear ones, and she gave out a few pairs of them today, and she's giving out some more tomorrow, but she gave a few pairs to me for Beaker. 'Meep! If you want, I bought you some new science goggles,' said Sophie's mom and Sophie. 'Meep! Meep!'
'And Bunsen,' said Sophie's mom, 'I've got a gift for you. It's new laboratory goggles.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: They're green.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: They're beautiful! Well, green is a great choice for me, says Bunsen, because like my name, Honeydew, I'm all green.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well, well, they have a green framing and a clear see-through part. And Beaker, I bought you—and Sophie said, 'I bought you orange-framed laboratory goggles that have a clear part for you to see through. I bought you them!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Meep!' says Beaker. 'I love them! I bought you the new kind that are just your size.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Meep!' That means thank you.
So everyone put on their safety goggles, and Bunsen said, 'But wait, what are we doing for our experiment today, Sophie? What question do you have?'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Mixing with droppers! But mixing colors with droppers.
Wonderful. Now, let's get out our colors and our droppers and our water. And so Bunsen and Beaker got out lots and lots of beautiful food coloring and droppers...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And we liked four bowls.
And four bowls and some water.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And four ounces of water. And four waters, and um, I would like one color purple, one color red, one color pink, one color green. So it's one purple, one drop of purple, one drop of pink, one drop of red, one drop of blue, one drop of green. Is that four?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That was five, actually, says Bunsen, but that's alright.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah! Now we're going to squirt them and then mix them, said Sophie and Sophie's mom.

Oh, very interesting. Now, I wonder if you could use what we learn in our color-mixing experiments here to create new colors for your frosting at the bakery? Do you think it could be useful in that way?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: We make frosting a different way, by making it ooey-gooey.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, but how do you make it different colors?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: By putting um some food coloring in it. And Sophie decides which color, and then I put a drop for the frosting, and then I mix it and mix it and bring it to the bakery and put it on my icing cake that I started this morning, and I put a cherry on top and it had chocolate.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That sounds wonderful. Hmm, well, is there anything you're curious about about how colors mix?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.
What would you like to know?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: What makes green? What makes purple and what makes pink? What colors make all those colors?
Okay, green, purple, and pink, we can surely do that, says Bunsen.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Green, purple, and pink.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Now, do you have any guesses about green? I think you might already know this one.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, oh yeah! I discovered that orange and blue make green... teal-y green. Orange and blue make teal-y green and blue and yellow make green.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Bunsen said, 'Oh, very interesting, yes, I see.'
Oh yeah, and who knows what purple and pink colors need to make purple and pink?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, do you know how to make purple out of two other colors, says Bunsen?
Nope!
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, here's some blue and here's some red, and why don't you try those and see what you get?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Purple!
Ah, very nice. Now, how could you make that a lighter purple? Hmm.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: By adding less.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Adding less, or we could put more water and it would be lighter in color.
No, I like dark purple.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Me too, dark purple can be very nice. Now, what about pink though?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know any colors for pink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, do you have a guess about what we could mix together to make pink?
Red and... red and green, I think.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, let's try mixing red and green and see what we get, that's a great experiment. Here's some red and here's some green. And Sophie mixed them together and you know what she saw?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It turned kind of brown.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I really want to make pink.

Hmm, well, let's just try again. That's what experimenting is all about, you just keep trying until you find out the thing that you're interested in knowing, or until you notice something new and interesting. And so Bunsen said, 'Well, here's some red and here's some white. Let's mix those together.'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Pink!
Pink! Yes, that's perfect.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: It's light pink. It's light pink though. Would you like it to be light pink?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That's perfect, says Bunsen. It's a beautiful color. But how would we make a dark pink, do you think?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Um, I don't know. Let's try blue and orange. Blue and orange.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Hmm, well, if we add blue and orange, and Bunsen added a drop of blue and a drop of orange, that will make it sort of brown, but a kind of light brown. That's very interesting. Well, there's lots of good experiments to do, aren't there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I discovered it was pretty teal-y.

Oh, I see. Well, that's all the time we have for color experiments today. Do you want to do any other experiments or maybe we could go to the bakery and you could teach me something about your recipes there?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh yes! I have like meat salad cupcakes, and sprinkle chocolate cakes, and orange juice berry cakes, and blueberry flavored cupcakes, and strawberry mixable, like gluten-free for Beaker labeling mugs, and coffee cup and coffees and like cups for water that are pink, purple, and yellow. Blue and pink, blue and yellow are the coffee cups. The water cups are blue, yellow, and pink.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, let's go over there. And Bunsen and Beaker and Sophie Mouse and her mom went over to the bakery and Bunsen said, 'Would you be willing to show me—'
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And Sophie said, 'My dad is the architect! I'm going to go visit him soon.'
Oh good. Would you be willing to show me how you make one of these kinds of delicious baked goods? You can pick one and tell me the recipe and how you make it.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh yeah. What about the pork tortilla cupcakes? So we add a tortilla and shape it into a cupcake, put sprinkles in it and then cover them up with frosting, and then put the meat on and then cupcake and then make it like a cupcake shape and then bake it in the oven and then it's ready to put in the bakery.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that sounds delicious. Thanks for teaching me about that. And Beaker said, 'Meep meep meep!' and that's the end of Stars and Stories.
No, I'm going to take out the light... Just so you're going to turn on the phone when you turn on the light. You're going to turn off the phone when I turn on the light.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah. Okay. I'll do it. Very good.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Bunsen Honeydew
characters.description: A green scientist known as Honeydew who owns a science laboratory decorated with skeletons and chemical jars.

characters.name: Beaker
characters.description: A high-strung Muppet and devoted companion to Bunsen, this jittery assistant—and close friend to Kitty—communicates solely through a frantic cadence of staccato "meeps" and "me-me" sounds. Driven by a nervous obsession with their work, they share a home with the scientist, remaining perpetually wide-eyed and sleepless until the day’s experiments reach a successful conclusion, pausing only for the occasional, coveted treat from the local bakery.

characters.name: Sophie
characters.description: The resilient owner and artisan of the Eat and Greet Bakery is a vibrant pink mouse with an iconic, rounded silhouette, typically found in a pink dress and a flour-dusted blue apron. A master of alchemical confections, she kneads life into glowing pink slime alongside her loyal companion, Wenden—a slime mouse who studies the science of her color-mixing. Her delicate appearance belies a legendary endurance, famously proven when she carried her brother Winston through the freezing wilds to the gates of Snow World.

characters.name: Sophie's Mom
characters.description: A helpful mouse who provides laboratory goggles for Sophie, Bunsen, and Beaker.

characters.name: Sophie's Dad
characters.description: An architect who designed the Eat and Greet Bakery.

places.name: The Science Lab
places.description: A room containing a world map, a human skeleton, and various jars of chemicals for mixing.

places.name: Sophie's Bakery
places.description: A shop known for making delicious treats.
